confirmed.where: App/syntax/gosyntaxplugin.cpp:33-110 vs App/syntax/rustsyntaxplugin.cpp:33-120 (and 8 more sibling plugins)
confirmed.note: 10 syntax plugins added at once: syntaxRules() keyword-loop scaffold + number/string/char/function/comment SyntaxRule blocks + multiLineBlocks() + keywords() are verbatim/near-verbatim copies across all plugins; only the keyword data-tables and a few language-specific rules diverge

QFile open + readAll + close + QJsonDocument::fromJson + parseError-check block (~14 lines) copy-pasted verbatim across loadBuiltInTemplates / loadUserTemplates / loadAssignmentsFromDir in the same file

false_positives.where: App/ui/uistylehelper.cpp:809-865 (primaryButtonStyle) vs 867-925 (secondaryButtonStyle) and ~20 sibling xStyle() methods
false_positives.what_happened: Each UIStyleHelper::xStyle() returns QString("<QtWidget> { ... }").arg(...).arg(...). Token stream is dominated by the identical Qt-stylesheet skeleton (selector + property keywords + %N placeholders + chained .arg()). A token/line detector sees ~70% shared tokens and flags them as clones, but the load-bearing payload (the CSS rules and which colors map to which placeholder) differs per method — it is a per-widget style data-table, not duplicated logic. Also each method exists as two overloads (Theme& legacy + ThemeDefinition& new) in this refactor, inflating apparent duplication.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: add a 'string-literal-dominated body' guard — when a candidate clone's shared tokens are >X% inside string/raw-string literals (CSS/SQL/format-template content), down-weight or drop it; complementary: treat a run of N consecutive .arg()/builder calls as a single normalized token so method-to-method .arg chains don't dominate similarity. Tune existing selective-normalization to collapse Qt-stylesheet QString literals to a placeholder before scoring.

false_positives.what_happened: load/save mirror pair: same configDir/configFile derivation, same QJsonObject/QJsonArray field names ("file","template","customArgs","customEnv"), same iterate-and-map idiom. Shared identifiers and Qt-JSON idiom create high token overlap, but control flow is inverse (read-into-struct vs struct-into-write) so it is a serializer/deserializer idiom, not copy-pasted logic.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: detect inverse-direction serializer pairs — if two candidate clones share the same string-literal key set but one is read-dominated (toString/toArray/value()) and the other write-dominated (insert/append/operator[]=), classify as serializer-pair and suppress. Cheaper heuristic: require matching statement-order direction (assignment LHS vs RHS source) before confirming a clone.
